channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: EC launches detailed investigation into Saipem and Subsea 7 merger
item.description: The European Commission (EC) has initiated an in-depth probe into the previously announced merger between rival offshore engineering and construction services providers Saipem and Subsea 7.

item.title: Vallourec to supply pipes for Atapu 2 offshore project in Brazil
item.description: Vallourec has secured a contract from Allseas to supply carbon steel seamless line pipes and thermal insulation coating for the Atapu 2 offshore oil development in Brazil.

item.title: Oil Prices Climb Toward $100 as Red Sea Risks Rise
item.description: Oil prices rose for the fifth consecutive day early on Thursday, topping $98 per barrel to the highest level in nearly two months, as Houthi attacks on tankers on the Red Sea route intensified concerns about supply from…

item.title: EU Concedes to Greek LNG Demand in Russian Sanctions Row
item.description: The European Union will exempt Greek Dynagas from the latest sanctions package for Russia after pressure from the Greek government, which said the package would harm the country’s economy. The package, set to be voted…

item.title: India’s Solar Manufacturing Push Backfires as Panel Factories Shut Down
item.description: India ran into an obstacle in its ambitions to boost solar generation as local factories began to shut down because of component shortages, Reuters reported today, citing unnamed industry sources. The shortages are the…

item.title: Two Saudi Oil Tankers Targeted as Houthi Blockade Disrupts Red Sea Shipping
item.description: At least two tankers have turned away from the Bab el-Mandeb Strait after Yemen’s Houthis said they have struck two Saudi tankers in the chokepoint in the latest Middle East war escalation, saying they had violated the…

item.title: Brent Breaks $96 as U.S. Strikes Iran for 12th Consecutive Night
item.description: The latest oil price rally showed no sign of stopping in early Asian trading on Thursday, with both benchmarks climbing to their highest levels in more than six weeks as the U.S. and Iran continued to exchange strikes…

item.title: Global Refiners Are Cutting Out Oil Traders To Buy Venezuelan Crude Directly
item.description: Commodity traders are having the rug pulled out from under one of their biggest paydays yet as refiners start bypassing oil and traders and buying Venezuelan crude directly, according to Reuters. Refiners and major…

item.title: New Pipelines Set To Ease Permian Natural Gas Glut
item.description: The regional price of natural gas produced in the Permian, the top U.S. oil basin, was negative for most of the first half of the year. Rising associated gas output from oil-targeting wells has had nowhere to go…

item.title: Equinor CEO: Europe May Miss Winter Gas Storage Goal
item.description: Europe's effort to rebuild natural gas inventories before winter suffered another setback on Wednesday after Equinor CEO Anders Opedal warned the region may struggle to reach its 80% storage target, highlighting…

item.title: Houthi Red Sea Blockade Could Shatter Hopes for Lower Oil Prices
item.description: Renewed Houthi blockade of Saudi Red Sea ports threatens up to 4 million bpd of redirected Saudi crude exports, adding fresh supply risks as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively disrupted The latest reignition of…

item.title: MIT Framework Maps Grid Weak Spots Before Climate Disasters Hit
item.description: Researchers at MIT have developed a new framework to map out geographical hotspots and pain points for our beleaguered electrical grids. As extreme weather events and soaring summer heat waves become more commonplace…

item.title: Asia to dominate global propylene capacity additions by 2030
item.description: Asia is poised to dominate the global propylene capacity additions by 2030 to satisfy surging domestic demand for end-use products.

item.title: North America leads global SAF production capacity growth through 2030
item.description: The US remains the key growth engine for the region, accounting for the majority of North America's upcoming SAF production capacity.

item.title: Africa’s Richest Man Proposes To Build 700,000 Bpd Oil Refinery In Kenya
item.description: East Africa has spent decades exporting one commodity and importing another. The region holds roughly 4.7 billion barrels of crude oil reserves and more than 70 trillion cubic feet of natural gas across Uganda, South…

item.title: Eni Côte d’Ivoire selects Saipem for $260m offshore drilling project
item.description: Italian oilfield services company Saipem has secured a new offshore drilling contract valued at around $260m from Eni Côte d’Ivoire.

item.title: The Metals Selloff Is Creating New Winners And Losers
item.description: Metals markets remain in a delicate tug-of-war, with structural deficits and massive AI-driven data center infrastructure buildout providing forward momentum, while global growth reassessments, high energy prices and…

item.title: China’s AI Offensive Gains Ground in Central Asia
item.description: Central Asian states are tilting towards Beijing amid intensifying competition between the United States and China for predominance in artificial intelligence development. Top officials from Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan…

item.title: GE Vernova Raises Outlook As AI Power Boom Masks Wind Weakness
item.description: GE Vernova raised its full-year revenue forecast on Wednesday after another quarter of booming demand for gas turbines, grid equipment and AI-driven electricity infrastructure. Wind was the clear exception. The…

item.title: Brazil’s Oil Boom Is Accelerating as Asian Buyers Flee the Middle East
item.description: Since Middle East hostilities reignited, oil prices have soared higher, although they remain below the highs witnessed during April 2026. This can be blamed on U.S. strikes against Iran, with Tehran and Washington…
